videos.title: Five principles for trusted communication during disasters | UNDRR
videos.description:

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: AI for Clean Air: Scaling Smarter Solutions
videos.description: Air pollution is one of the most pressing development challenges globally, with far-reaching impacts on public health, economic growth, climate resilience, and sustainable development. videos.title: 知識から実践へ: 緊急事態への備えと対応アカデミー (日本)
videos.description:
2026年4月、世界銀行東京防災ハブと防災グローバルファシリティ(GFDRR)は、日本政府およびカナダ政府の財政的支援のもと、「緊急事態への備えと対応(EP&R: Emergency Preparedness and Response)アカデミー」を東京と仙台で開催しました。
 
このアカデミーは参加者同士の学び合い・技術交流の場として開かれ、世界銀行加盟15カ国の政府代表者、世界銀行のオペレーションチーム、そして日本の専門家が一堂に会し、制度の比較や教訓の共有を通じて課題解決に取り組みました

ABOUT THE GLOBAL FACILITY FOR DISASTER REDUCTION

videos.title: From Knowledge to Action: EP&R Learning Academy in Japan
videos.description:
In April 2026, the World Bank Tokyo DRM Hub and GFDRR hosted the EP&R Learning Academy in Tokyo and Sendai, Japan, with financial support from the Government of Japan and the Government of Canada.

videos.title: Children in Nepal are learning to survive floods in virtual reality | UNDRR
videos.description:

Inside a digital version of their own school, they explore:

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.description:

videos.title: Expanding Mozambique’s infrastructure to drive sustainable long-term growth (Portuguese version)
videos.description: ABOUT THE GLOBAL FACILITY FOR DISASTER REDUCTION AND RECOVERY (GFDRR) is a global partnership that helps low- and lower-middle-income countries better understand and reduce their vulnerabilities to natural hazards and adapt to climate change. videos.title: What is the value of the Stakeholder Engagement Mechanism? | UNDRR
videos.description:
🌍 Disaster risk reduction cannot happen in isolation.

Across the world, communities, organizations, businesses, and governments are working to reduce disaster risk. But when efforts remain fragmented, their impact can fall short.

That’s where the Stakeholder Engagement Mechanism (SEM) comes in.

🤝
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: Did you know 2025 saw the 2nd-highest tsunami wave on record? | UNDRR
videos.description:
In August 2025, a landslide into Alaska's Tracy Arm fjord generated the second-highest tsunami on record — sending water 481 metres up the opposite wall. No ships were nearby... but a cruise ship had visited the fjord just 12 hours before. 🛳️
 
The underlying driver was climate-related glacier retre
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How does investment in resilience reduce risk in Greater Mumbai? | UNDRR
videos.description: In Greater Mumbai, targeted resilience measures could avoid more than US$250 million annually in projected climate-related property damage by mid-century.
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How and why stakeholder engagement matters for disaster risk reduction | UNDRR
videos.description:
Disaster risk reduction is not just the responsibility of governments.
Building resilience requires an all-of-society approach—bringing together communities, civil society, businesses, academia, and decision-makers.

💡 Why?
Because the people closest to risk are often closest to the solutions.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: Strengthening coastal resilience in Saint Louis, Senegal
videos.description:
Discover how the "Saint Louis Emergency Recovery and Resilience" Project has has a beneficial impact on communities threated by the hazard of coastal erosion in the city of Saint-Louis, Senegal.

The ACP-EU NDRR Program's "Strengthening Urban and Coastal Resilience" project also supported this initi

videos.title: Strengthening Tanzania’s infrastructure and services for its citizens
videos.description:
To keep up with rapid urban growth, Tanzania is integrating green infrastructure into its development plans. By balancing new construction with Nature-Based Solutions, the country seeks to curb rising temperatures and strengthen its resilience against floods and droughts.

ABOUT THE GLOBAL FACILITY

videos.title: Why undersea cables are critical infrastructure | UNDRR
videos.description:
🌐 The internet depends on cables hidden deep beneath the ocean.

Undersea cables carry 99% of global internet traffic, making them one of the world’s most critical pieces of infrastructure.

⚠️ But when disaster strikes, the impacts can be huge.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction
